free_text: Measurement of the total neutron cross-section of
selenium at neutron energies below 2 eV
Numerical data for total sig.
Two samples of thickness 2.08 g/cm2 and 1.36 g/cm2
used. Prepared from a spec fine powder(grain size less
than 10**(-4) cm.). Spectroscopic analysis of selenium:
Selenium crystalizes in hexagonal system with cell
constants a0 = 4.954 Angstrom related to the space
group d(4)(3)-p3(1)-21 with the position of the atoms
located at (x,0,1/3,    0,x,2/3,    x(bar),x(bar),0).
Absolute transmission measurement.
ET-RR-1 reactor
Transmission method in connection with a
time-of-flight spectrometer.
Mechanical chopper
He3 gas filled neutron detector, placed at
the end of 8.1 m flight path.
No description of error analysis.
Given data is taken from Jour. of
Atomkernenergie. Kerntechnik vol. 42(1983) No.3, p.187.
TMK
SD:Updated to new date formats,lower case.
Fig. of cross-section vs. neutron wavelength and energy
given in jour. Atomkernenergie.Kerntechnik vol. 42
(1983) No.3, p.187. At neutron energies between 2.2 eV
and 0.2 eV, the data were fitted by Least Square
method. The expression obtained for the thermal total
cross section was found to be
Given data obtained by private communication,
M.Salama, 26 June 1988. This data correspond to fig. 1
of Atomkernenergie, vol. 42(1983) No. 3
The given data is in good agreement within statistical
error with that of (21.40 +- 1.00) b of Mughabghab,
S.F., D.I.Garber: BNL - 325(1976)
The given data is in good agreement within statistical
error with the value (12.1 +- 0.30) b of Pomerance,
H.: Phys. Rev. 83 (1951) 641 - 645) using the pile
oscillator method, also in agreement within
statistical error with the value (11.80 +- 0.40) b of
Hughes,D.J., R.Schwartz: Neutron Cross-Sections,
BNL-325.,to be slightly higher than the values
(11.50 +- 0.10) b of Lapointe,C.,F.Rasetti: Phys. Rev.
Meadows,J.W., J.F.Wolem: Nucl. Sci. and Eng. 9 (1961)
Comment by compiler
Data given in Table and discussion is slightly
different. Given in Table is (12.29 +- 0.31) b.
Given data is obtained after applying the correction
factor term (A+1/A)**2.
Given data was found to be in good agreement within
statistical errors with the value (9 +- 1) b of
Egelstaff,P.A.: AERE, N/R 1147 (1958) and that of
(8.00 +- 1.00) of Hughes,D.J., R.Schwartz: Neutron
Cross-Sections, BNL- 325, 2nd ed. (1958), Supplement
(1960) and Supplement no. 2(1966)  Slightly higher
than the sig(S)(free) values of Koester,L.,K.Knoph,
W.Waschowski: Z. Phys. A 296, 1(1980)43., after
being corrected by (A+1/A)**2.
Comment by compiler:
Data given in Table and discussion is slightly
different. given in Table is (8.66 +- 0.25) b.
Author designated as average scattering cross section
Given data was found to be in agreement within
statistical errors with the value of (9.70 +- 1.0) b
of Mughabghab,S.F.,D.I.Garber: BNL-325(1976) and in
reasonable agreement within statistical errors with
that of (11.00 +- 2.00) b. Noticeable disagreement with
the value of (13.00 +- 1.00) b of neutron cross
sections, AECU 2040 (Office of Technical Services,
Oak Ridge, Tenn.) (1952). (7.70+- 0.08) b of  Koester,
L., K.Knoph, W.Waschowski: Z.Phys. A296,1(1980) 43
seems to be lower than the given value
Given data is in agreement within statistical errors
with (7.47 +- 4.00) F of Egelstaff, P.A.: AERE,
N/R 1147 (1958) and also with that of (8.10 +- 0.05) F
of Mughabghab,S.F., D.I.Garber: BNL-325 (1976).
also in agreement within statistical errors with
(7.97 +- 0.009) F of Koester, L.K.Knoph,
W.Waschowski: Z.Phys. A296, 1(1980)43
Given data is in agreement within statistical errors
with (7.00 +- 2.00) b of Egelstaff,P.A.: AERE,
N/R 1147 (1958) and also with that of (8.24 +- 0.0003)b
of Mughabghab,S.F., D.I.Garber: BNL-325(1976). Also in
agreement within statistical errors with
(7.88 +- 0.001) b of Koester,L., K.Knoph, W.Waschowski
The error in the estimation of this value is large
and of the order of the cross section value, therefore
the incoherent scattering cross section could be
neglected. It is good agreement with isotopical
incoherent cross section of ordinary selenium
(9.32 +- 0.10) b and total incoherent bound
cross section of selenium (0.35 +- 0.10) b of
Koester L., K.Knoph, W.Waschowski: Z.Phys. A296,
Given data is slightly higher than that 5.69 F obtained
from optical model calculations for spherical nuclei.
Also compared with 7.03 F, which was calculated based
on averaging the potential scattering radii for
selenium isotopes and which was obtained by Koester
et al. from vibrational-rotational optical model
calculations of Jain,A.P.: Nucl. Phys.50 (1964) 157.
The comparison showed that the given data favours more
that calculated based on vibrational rotational
optical model than that calculated using spherical
optical model parameters.
